metadata.summary: Helm charts package Kubernetes resources for production deployment. Every chart must include HPA, PodDisruptionBudget, pod anti-affinity, health probes, resource limits, and non-root security context.

content.title: Helm Charts
content.summary: Helm is the package manager for Kubernetes (v4+ as of 2026). Charts bundle related K8s resources into configurable, versioned packages. Every production chart must include: resource requests/limits, HPA, PodDisruptionBudget, pod anti-affinity, all three health probes, and a non-root security context.

why.p: A 2025 analysis of 100+ open-source Helm charts found only 17% met production-reliability criteria (PDB, anti-affinity, HPA). Authoring charts with these elements from the start avoids emergency retrofits under load. values.schema.json validation catches misconfiguration before apply.

list.item: Complex applications with many K8s resources across multiple environments
Reusable deployment packages shared across teams or clusters
GitOps workflows (ArgoCD, Flux) where charts are the unit of change
Multi-environment deploys with per-environment values-{env}.yaml files
Quick experiments or learning — plain YAML is simpler
Small, static setups with no variation across environments — use Kustomize
When you want Helm to manage secrets — use External Secrets Operator instead

rule.statement: Set apiVersion: v2, include kubeVersion constraint, list all maintainers, and use SemVer 2 for version.
rule.rationale: apiVersion v2 is required for Helm 3 features (dependencies, library charts). kubeVersion prevents deployment to incompatible clusters.

example.code: apiVersion: v2 name: myapp description: A Helm chart for MyApp type: application version: 1.0.0 appVersion: "2.0.0" kubeVersion: ">=1.25.0" maintainers: - name: Platform Team email: platform@example.com dependencies: - name: postgresql version: "12.x.x" repository: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami condition: postgresql.enabled

rule.statement: Include templates/NOTES.txt, templates/_helpers.tpl, and templates/tests/test-connection.yaml in every chart.
rule.rationale: NOTES.txt shows access instructions post-install. _helpers.tpl centralizes naming logic. Test pod verifies the release works end-to-end.

rule.statement: Add values.schema.json to validate user inputs at helm install/upgrade time.
rule.rationale: Schema validation catches type errors and missing required values before Kubernetes rejects manifests, surfacing errors with clear messages.

rule.statement: Chart name: lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only; starts with a letter; no dots or underscores. Template files: {chart}-{resource}.yaml.
rule.rationale: Kubernetes name constraints propagate from chart names. Consistent template naming makes navigation predictable.

rule.statement: Pin dependency versions (never use wildcard *). Add condition: dep.enabled flag. Commit Chart.lock. Run helm dependency update before packaging.
rule.rationale: Wildcards silently pull breaking changes. Condition flags allow disabling subcharts for minimal installs. Lock file ensures reproducible builds.

example.code: # Bad: wildcard version, no condition flag dependencies: - name: redis version: "*" repository: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

rule.statement: All configurable values must be in values.yaml. No hardcoded values in templates. Document every value with a comment.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded values prevent per-environment customization and make charts unusable by others.

rule.statement: Never put secrets in values.yaml. Use External Secrets Operator or reference existing Kubernetes secrets.
rule.rationale: values.yaml is committed to version control and passed in plain text to helm upgrade; secrets must not be stored there.

rule.statement: Set HPA minReplicas >= 2. Never set minReplicas to 1 for production services.
rule.rationale: minReplicas=1 defeats high availability: when the single pod is evicted or the node is drained, the service has zero replicas until HPA reacts.

rule.statement: Define PodDisruptionBudget with minAvailable=2 (or maxUnavailable=1) for all stateless services.
rule.rationale: Without PDB, node drain during upgrades can evict all replicas simultaneously causing outage. PDB prevents the cluster from removing too many pods at once.

rule.statement: Configure pod anti-affinity (soft by default, hard for critical services) to spread pods across nodes and zones.
rule.rationale: Without anti-affinity, the scheduler may place all replicas on one node; a node failure takes the entire service down.

rule.statement: Define all three probes: livenessProbe, readinessProbe, and startupProbe for slow-starting applications.
rule.rationale: livenessProbe restarts deadlocked containers. readinessProbe prevents traffic to pods not yet ready. startupProbe prevents liveness from killing slow-start containers.

rule.statement: Always define resource requests and limits. Use configChecksum annotations to trigger pod restart when ConfigMap or Secret changes.
rule.rationale: Missing requests break scheduling decisions. Without checksum annotations, config changes require manual rollout.

rule.statement: Set runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, and drop ALL capabilities in securityContext.
rule.rationale: Root containers can escape to the host. Read-only filesystem prevents runtime modification. Capability drop minimizes attack surface.

example.code: securityContext: allowPrivilegeEscalation: false readOnlyRootFilesystem: true runAsNonRoot: true runAsUser: 1000 capabilities: drop: - ALL

rule.statement: Enable NetworkPolicy to restrict ingress to only ingress-controller and same-namespace pods. Allow egress for DNS (UDP 53) and same-namespace pods.
rule.rationale: Without NetworkPolicy, any compromised pod in the cluster can reach your service directly, bypassing ingress rules.

rule.statement: Create a dedicated ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false unless the application needs the K8s API.
rule.rationale: Default service account token is mounted automatically and can be used for cluster API access if the container is compromised.

rule.statement: Always use --atomic flag for helm upgrade in CI/CD. Set --timeout appropriately. Configure rolling update strategy.
rule.rationale: --atomic rolls back automatically on failure, preventing partial upgrades. Without it, a failed upgrade leaves the release in a broken state.

antipattern.description: Using helm upgrade without --atomic in CI/CD.
antipattern.reason: A failed upgrade without --atomic leaves the release in a degraded state requiring manual intervention. --atomic ensures clean rollback.

section.name: Production values.yaml
section.p: Key sections of a production-ready values.yaml. All values must be commented. Security context and HPA are required.

example.code:
# -- Replicas (ignored if autoscaling.enabled=true) replicaCount: 3
image: repository: myapp tag: "" # defaults to Chart.appVersion pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
podSecurityContext: runAsNonRoot: true runAsUser: 1000 fsGroup: 1000 seccompProfile: type: RuntimeDefault
securityContext: allowPrivilegeEscalation: false readOnlyRootFilesystem: true runAsNonRoot: true runAsUser: 1000 capabilities: drop: [ALL]
autoscaling: enabled: true minReplicas: 3 # never 1 in production maxReplicas: 10 targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 70
podDisruptionBudget: enabled: true minAvailable: 2
podAntiAffinity: enabled: true type: soft # hard for critical services
resources: requests: { cpu: 100m, memory: 256Mi } limits: { cpu: 500m, memory: 512Mi }

# values-dev.yaml — minimal, no HA replicaCount: 1 autoscaling: enabled: false podDisruptionBudget: enabled: false resources: requests: { cpu: 50m, memory: 128Mi } limits: { cpu: 200m, memory: 256Mi }

# values-prod.yaml — full HA replicaCount: 3 autoscaling: enabled: true minReplicas: 3 maxReplicas: 20 podAntiAffinity: enabled: true type: hard resources: requests: { cpu: 500m, memory: 512Mi } limits: { cpu: 2000m, memory: 2Gi }

rule.statement: Define values.schema.json with types, descriptions, and minimum/maximum constraints for numeric fields. Mark image.repository as required.
rule.rationale: Schema validation catches wrong types (string instead of integer) and missing required values at helm install time rather than at runtime.

template.purpose: Standard Go template helpers: name, fullname, labels, selectorLabels, image, configChecksum
Production deployment template with all reliability and security fields
LLM prompt to create a production-ready Helm chart
